CREATE TABLE regression_data (id INT PRIMARY KEY, expr1 FLOAT, expr2 FLOAT);
INSERT INTO regression_data (id, expr1, expr2) VALUES (1, 3.5, 2.5);
INSERT INTO regression_data (id, expr1, expr2) VALUES (2, NULL, 3.0);
INSERT INTO regression_data (id, expr1, expr2) VALUES (3, 4.0, 5.0);
INSERT INTO regression_data (id, expr1, expr2) VALUES (4, 7.5, NULL);
INSERT INTO regression_data (id, expr1, expr2) VALUES (5, 5.0, 4.0);
SELECT REGR_AVGX(expr2) FROM regression_data WHERE expr1 IS NOT NULL AND expr2 IS NOT NULL;
SELECT AVG(expr2) AS average_expr2, REGR_AVGX(expr2) FROM regression_data WHERE expr1 IS NOT NULL GROUP BY expr1;
SELECT d1.id, d1.expr1, (SELECT REGR_AVGX(d2.expr2) FROM regression_data d2 WHERE d2.expr1 = d1.expr1) AS avg_expr2 FROM regression_data d1 WHERE d1.expr1 IS NOT NULL;
CREATE TABLE additional_data (data_id INT PRIMARY KEY, expr1 FLOAT, expr2 FLOAT);
INSERT INTO additional_data (data_id, expr1, expr2) VALUES (1, 2.0, 2.5);
INSERT INTO additional_data (data_id, expr1, expr2) VALUES (2, 3.5, 3.5);
SELECT d1.id, REGR_AVGX(d2.expr2) FROM regression_data d1 JOIN additional_data d2 ON d1.expr1 = d2.expr1 WHERE d1.expr1 IS NOT NULL AND d2.expr2 IS NOT NULL GROUP BY d1.id;
SELECT REGR_AVGX(expr2) FROM (SELECT expr2 FROM regression_data WHERE expr1 > 0) AS filtered_data;
SELECT REGR_AVGX(expr2) OVER (PARTITION BY expr1) FROM regression_data WHERE expr1 IS NOT NULL;
